author: soumith
text: switch CUDA svd and qr to using cuSolver

author: soumith
text:
Currently we use MAGMA for these solvers, which uses a mix of CPU and GPU. Wonder if cuSolver will be faster, and we can avoid the CPU path entirely.

cc: [@ngimel](https://github.com/ngimel) just want to confirm, cuSolver (dense) wont use the CPU right?

cc [@ngimel](https://github.com/ngimel) [@vincentqb](https://github.com/vincentqb) [@vishwakftw](https://github.com/vishwakftw) [@ssnl](https://github.com/ssnl) [@jianyuh](https://github.com/jianyuh)

author: ngimel
text: Checked with cuSolver team, qr is GPU only, for svd they have CPU path (gesvd) and GPU-only path (gesvdj). They don't benchmark against magma, so YMMV.

author: PiotrSokol
text:
[@soumith](https://github.com/soumith)  
I'm sorry to go a bit off topic but I can't find reference about using gpu supported svd/qr. Is it possible to force MAGMA to run the factorization on the gpu?

author: ssnl
text: [@PiotrSokol](https://github.com/PiotrSokol) MAGMA's implementation of qr and svd has CPU calls. There are no alternative in MAGMA for these calls afaik. So no.

author: PiotrSokol
text:
[@ssnl](https://github.com/ssnl) Thanks!

[@soumith](https://github.com/soumith) Concerning the benchmarking, cupy uses cusolver as a backend and I compared it, though not very dilligently, to pytorch/MAGMA. MAGMA is ~2.5 faster on my setup, which is a 46 core Xeon CPU E5-2690 v4 @ 2.60GHz and a Tesla P100 GPU.

author: lezcano
text: [CUDA][Linalg] Add gesvd as SVD fallback; optimize SVD gesvdj performance

author: IvanYashchuk
text: cuSOLVER paths for QR and SVD for CUDA 10.2+ builds were added with [#56256](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/56256) and [#48436](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/48436).
